mapping.a_element: galaxy/LRG/QSO angular positions treated as a clustering point pattern
mapping.b_element: mini-supermarket store locations modeled as a log-Gaussian Cox point process

mapping.a_element: underlying dark-matter density field whose clustering the tracers sample
mapping.b_element: latent Gaussian-process spatial effect shared by the two model components

mapping.a_element: tracer bias b linking galaxy/QSO overdensity to the mass field
mapping.b_element: loading parameter linking the shared latent field to the survival outcome

mapping.a_element: ACT CMB-lensing convergence map, a second field tracing the same mass
mapping.b_element: binary store-survival probit outcome, a second observable driven by the same latent field

mapping.a_element: galaxy x lensing cross-power spectrum P_gK used to infer the coupling
mapping.b_element: shared-GP term in the joint LGCP+probit likelihood measuring the placement-survival association

brief_justification: Both treat a spatial point pattern (galaxy/QSO positions; mini-supermarket locations) as a Poisson/Cox process whose intensity is modulated by a latent Gaussian random field (the dark-matter density field probed by CMB lensing; the LGCP's spatial GP), and both use a single scalar coupling parameter -- tracer bias b in A, loading in B -- to quantify how strongly a second co-located observable (CMB-lensing convergence; store survival) shares that same latent field, inferred from their cross-correlation (P_gK; joint-likelihood loading). The transferable machinery is the biased-tracer / log-Gaussian Cox process framework common to both.
